canto_title: The Second Circle and the Wood of Suicides

scenes.summary: Dante and Virgil enter a dark, twisted forest inhabited by the Harpies.
Virgil informs Dante that they have entered the second circle of Hell.
Dante is confused by disembodied cries and Virgil warns him not to touch the vegetation.
Dante breaks a twig from a plum tree, which begins to bleed and speak in pain.
The broken branch leaks both words and blood, leaving Dante terrified.
Virgil tells the speaking branch to identify himself to redeem his reputation.
The tree reveals he was once a man who held the secrets of Frederick II.
The speaker describes how his political intrigues and betrayal led to his downfall.
The tree swears he never broke faith with his lord, despite his current state.
Virgil urges Dante to ask the tree more questions to gain understanding.
Dante asks the spirit to explain the nature of the souls trapped in the trees.
The tree explains how Minos sends fierce souls to become trees that are eaten by Harpies.
A sudden, loud commotion like a hunting party interrupts the silence.
Two naked, wounded souls run frantically through the woods, fleeing death.
Black hounds emerge from the woods and tear the fleeing souls apart.
Virgil leads Dante to a broken bush that cries out in agony.
The wounded bush reveals his identity and laments the destruction of his Florentine home.
